hub_subtitle: Conversação para viagens, listening de verdade e reuniões internacionais — rumo a Nova York, setembro de 2026

lesson.menu_title: Who Is Lucimara? -- Diagnostic Session and Personal Introduction
lesson.menu_desc: Introducing yourself and your work at an international trade fair in New York -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 1 -- Diagnostic + Personal Introduction
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Lucimara | Aula 1 -- Diagnostic + Personal Introduction

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the International Chemical Trade Fair here in New York. Before we start, a few quick announcements. Registration is open until ten thirty at the main desk, and your badge gives you access to all three halls. The opening panel begins at eleven o'clock in Hall B, and this year we are recording every session, so you can watch it again later. If you are meeting a supplier today, please use the meeting rooms on the second floor. Lunch is served from twelve thirty. And one last thing. If you don't catch a name or a number during a talk, don't worry. The full program is in your bag. Enjoy the fair.

Hi Lucimara, this is Karen Miller from the event office in New York. I'm calling about your registration for September. Everything is confirmed. Your badge is ready and you can collect it at the main desk on the first morning. Two small things. First, we need a short bio, about three sentences. What you do, where you work, and how long you have been in the industry. Second, the organizers are planning a dinner for the international guests on the Tuesday, so please tell me if you are coming. If you have any questions, call me back this week. Thank you, and see you in New York.

tips: I'm a director in the chemical industry.
I'm responsible for production and regulatory affairs.

items.situation: "How long have you been in the industry?"
tips: I've worked in the chemical industry for twenty years.

items.situation: "Are you working on anything interesting at the moment?"
tips: Right now, I'm preparing a new product registration.
Happening now, not every day -- present continuous
Our headquarters is in Guarulhos, near São Paulo.
I live in Perdizes and my commute takes forty minutes.
I travel abroad every two or three months.
I go to a trade fair in Lyon every November.

items.situation: The speaker talks fast and you miss the last sentence.
tips: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Could you say it again more slowly?
Do you mean the meeting on Tuesday?
